name: mindy@dscc.org
subject: Every Democrat just voted against this (Planned Parenthood)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
The GOP just voted to make it harder for millions of women to access birth control and cancer screenings.
---
[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170330-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
Friend --

When the Republican effort to repeal the Affordable Care Act collapsed under the weight of their own incompetence, they went back to the drawing board.

And today, we saw their first effort to regroup.

**Their target? Planned Parenthood.**

Fifty Republican senators, with Vice President Pence casting the tie-breaking vote, advanced a new bill to strip away federal funding for the organization.

**Every Democrat in the Senate voted against this.**

[**We need to show we have their backs. Chip in $1 before tomorrow 's end-of-quarter deadline and your contribution will be triple-matched!**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170330-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

This isn't about abortion. Funding for abortion is already prohibited under federal law.

Instead, Republicans voted to move forward with a bill that will prevent Planned Parenthood from getting reimbursed for a whole range of medical services they offer.

In essence, the GOP just voted to make it harder for millions of women to access birth control and cancer screenings.

**And if this bill becomes law, the consequences will be far reaching.**

So before our deadline, I'm asking you to make one of the final contributions we need to hit our goal -- and show the Democrats in the Senate you have their back. Your donation will be triple-matched!

[**http://dscc.org/democrats-resist**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170330-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Thank you!

Mindy
DSCC Executive Director

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e28f/2b07114c/59a30884/736cb2fe/584465430/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e28f/2b07114c/59a30884/736cb2f9/584465430/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e28f/2b07114c/59a30884/736cb2f8/584465430/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

name: FL Democrats
subject: when we close the book:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Florida Democratic Party
body:
Friend:

Tomorrow at midnight, Democrats nationwide are going to be filing their quarterly Federal Election Commission (FEC) report that will show Democrats' fundraising totals. This is the first time we will show our hand and how we've mobilized to fight back.

**Here in Florida, we 've been fighting hard to stop Trump's agenda. But we're still about 9,419 donations short of our big March FEC deadline goal.**

If we're not able to hit this goal, it will give Trump the signal that he's going to be able to win Florida again in 2020.

name: Kate Black, EMILY's List
subject: BREAKING: They had to bring in Mike Pence >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d92/24c69fd9/f16199c3/18e389fc/1136295072/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact&source=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact")
---

Peter,

Senate Republicans went after women's health care AGAIN.

Earlier today they voted to repeal the protection President Obama put in place to prevent states from blocking funding for health care providers that perform abortions -- like Planned Parenthood.

This bill could have huge ramifications for health care clinics and force even more to close their doors.

Democratic opposition to this bill was so strong that Vice President Mike Pence had to be the tie-breaking vote to move the bill forward.

All 15 of our EMILY's List senators voted against it.

**More than half of our women in the Senate are up for re-election next year. Donate $3 to help hit our $180,000 End-of-Quarter goal so we can make sure they return and continue to _always_ defend women's health care:

[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Womens-Health-Care-Attack](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d92/24c69fd9/f16199c3/18e389fd/1136295072/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170330_email_EOQPPDefunding?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgWomensHealthCare&utm_campaign=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact&source=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact")**

Thanks,

Kate Black
Vice President of Research, EMILY's List

P.S. Just yesterday Pence was at a women's empowerment forum. Today he voted to disempower millions of women -- the first time in history a vice president has cast the tie-breaking vote to do so. **Want to fight Republican hypocrisy?[Donate $3 before midnight tomorrow so we can keep sending pro-choice Democratic women to fight back.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d92/24c69fd9/f16199c3/18e389fe/1136295072/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170330_email_EOQPPDefunding?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Donate+3+before+midnight+tomorrow+so+we+&utm_campaign=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact&source=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact") **

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d92/24c69fd9/f16199c3/18e389ff/1136295072/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170330_email_EOQPPDefunding?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact&source=WQEFE334N_digital_email_20170330_PPDefunding_inact")

|

---

---

name: The White House
subject: Your 1600 Daily
body:
Everything White House for 3/31/2017. |

**FEATURED**
Tune in TODAY at 10AM ET for President Donald J. Trump's Weekly Address to the Nation.
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5b53672f5eae32350a55230e591dda6bd3dfc12adc79c9ff5f441c4fa64b101daf44a3c3d3412167296ebe30b73aa7994e5131e2381799a1)

**WHITE HOUSE MEMO**
President Trump is taking action toward implementing an **America-first trade policy** , ensuring that every single one of our trade agreements increases our GDP growth rate, reduces our trade deficit, and strengthens our manufacturing base. The jobs are coming back, and this is only the beginning.

**MORNING:**
* **10:00AM** : President Trump meets with former Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice
* **11:00AM** : President Trump makes an announcement with the National Association of Manufacturers - **[Watch LIVE](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5b53672f5eae3235f0e481af9080206bd271151621362a350857cc27047c7b91f7373ad19b29e0f030c5578b0b9a475aeb0ba7668b12cc72)**

**AFTERNOON:**
* **3:00PM** : President Trump meets with the Director of the National Institutes of Health
* **4:45PM** : President Trump meets with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget

**GET INVOLVED**
Select your favorite photo of the day from the month of March. The winning photo will be featured on whitehouse.gov.
[See the Collection and Select Your Favorite Here](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=5b53672f5eae32351c70bb6e66c343a09249f32c7415f41b32db20e4bee3ae7cab4514e295daf6d8b41aaf7bf2ffa4e4533d9c4aaaacc417)

**FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP**
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2ae6d4af4cf1fd4e221a54d81da461b15cb2924c571d505a1edc0e023c12e9661ccae87391aaf10da52e242388153dbde)
[Retweet](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2612b380205a480532833b58e151b965438f83a2321b9afcf3848da3ce0208264327b2a600933449f974463c4c46fce41)

**OVAL OFFICE HIGHLIGHTS**
President Trump meets with Prime Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen of Denmark.
[Read More](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b27fc9898b0a527c14329bbf0e3e7ce3f5cdc92591d0f2ff5b69ed3a7a644b95188cee08ef99c8aa65410c6a2932e0657e)
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2d96cd82c43f621bcbca6efa199de2c18af30798af4eb73e4fd13e53dcad8f59fabdea8c40e8be4a19bc5fd9a02a50879)

**WHITE HOUSE UPDATES**
**Photo of the Day:**
[ ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b27af58a6daae913d60abc424905a0513bb16a56ace58f16da06401b663b50c100830aeb48800dd910602d8717d3537462)
President Donald Trump and Danish Prime Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen shake hands on Thursday, March 30, 2017, during a meeting in the Oval Office of the White House in Washington, D.C. (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen).
[View Photo](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b27af58a6daae913d60abc424905a0513bb16a56ace58f16da06401b663b50c100830aeb48800dd910602d8717d3537462)

President Trump eliminates job-killing regulations.
[Read More](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b238f5cd8969bd107618e53496bb3cdde18d3c467ed9cc499f92f87ca9b8b0c35d51bd1c9cab08a99a80a77d773deb055e)

Applications are being accepted for the White House Internship Program for 2017. The application period ends TODAY.
[Read More About the Program](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b27e9ed3f4ce069b79dfaa96b470d6205e2f35738caa6d06866d750146bfe0200e26bf54abe52640349ec69046a3bfabcf)
[Apply Here](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2a255c9f69e684da621388e1e0038af8b4d14fce2d54ddf0ed712b9a0dd5a76de0cd066d9cb0e7915d9acf452372e0a37)

**PRESS ROOM**
Watch yesterday's press briefing with Sean Spicer:
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2ca4f3132d81458c6cc7eb9447cb5f7d10152fc9f04f892938503bb472bb5a15f2927e5426d733ff73506aae3bed765cb)
[Read Transcript](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2143731bb7e8f1d281160ea394581aadeca015ae502f3ab0d462c27b1aa26b742470bfe133993f46a2d084cffdc12f91c)

Today's press briefing will take place at 1:00PM ET in the White House Briefing Room with Press Secretary Sean Spicer. [Watch it LIVE here.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2854be14ce8c9d101918ab9d87b715a3328071a80f20273587db88cdf0cb0987be5b676141353615d3b23955b28d9b0da)

**NEWS REPORTS**
* _The Washington Examiner:_ "Senate committee advances Acosta as labor secretary"
[Read More](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b2aaf9b16eb223e697d6560b903f24efecbe86a379b5a32d98a71fc6477e5e2949fd3dc61acde87a462a8a51be6e870cde)
* _The Hill:_ "Trump meets with Treasury Secretary during 'first stages' of tax reform"
[Read More](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=41c53486a2bf87b270c49e8d29a50bc1dcd7297b80d733523173d117f028dec4a636a65b6ad4bb3b566df6d8c8e9df5d0c20377a5334d87d)
* _Atlanta Journal-Constitution:_ "Sonny Perdue’s agriculture nomination clears key Senate hurdle"

clean_body: Everything White House for 3/31/2017. | FEATURED Tune in TODAY at 10AM ET for President Donald J. Trump's Weekly Address to the Nation. WHITE HOUSE MEMO President Trump is taking action toward implementing an America-first trade policy , ensuring that every single one of our trade agreements increases our GDP growth rate, reduces our trade deficit, and strengthens our manufacturing base. The jobs are coming back, and this is only the beginning. MORNING: 10:00AM : President Trump meets with former Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice 11:00AM : President Trump makes an announcement with the National Association of Manufacturers - AFTERNOON: 3:00PM : President Trump meets with the Director of the National Institutes of Health 4:45PM : President Trump meets with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget GET INVOLVED Select your favorite photo of the day from the month of March. The winning photo will be featured on whitehouse.gov. FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP OVAL OFFICE HIGHLIGHTS President Trump meets with Prime Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen of Denmark. WHITE HOUSE UPDATES Photo of the Day: President Donald Trump and Danish Prime Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen shake hands on Thursday, March 30, 2017, during a meeting in the Oval Office of the White House in Washington, D.C. (Official White House Photo by D. Myles Cullen). President Trump eliminates job-killing regulations. Applications are being accepted for the White House Internship Program for 2017. The application period ends TODAY. PRESS ROOM Watch yesterday's press briefing with Sean Spicer: Today's press briefing will take place at 1:00PM ET in the White House Briefing Room with Press Secretary Sean Spicer. NEWS REPORTS The Washington Examiner: "Senate committee advances Acosta as labor secretary" The Hill: "Trump meets with Treasury Secretary during 'first stages' of tax reform" * Atlanta Journal-Constitution: "Sonny Perdue’s agriculture nomination clears key Senate hurdle"

body:
We’re sending a powerful message. But need your help. |

Friend, TONIGHT at 11:59 is our FIRST end-of-quarter deadline. It’s your final chance to help us end the first quarter of our presidency as strong as possible. You know that CNN and MSNBC are dying to say that the Democrats beat us. They will use a bad report as reason to believe that our movement is finished. We need to set the record straight with an unbelievable first-quarter report that proves our movement is stronger than ever before. Just imagine when the talking heads over at MSNBC react to our incredible numbers on live TV. Please make a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $20, or even just $5 to help us post the best first quarter report in presidential history and PROVE THE MEDIA WRONG. Any amount will truly help, Friend. What matters most isn’t just the amount we raise -- but the number of individual Americans who step up to make a contribution to show their support. During the election, we raised more in small dollar donations than ANY candidate in history. More than Obama. More than Hillary. Even more than Bernie. It sent a message that our movement isn’t powered by a handful of major lobbyists, but by millions and millions of hardworking American citizens. Friend, you’re a part of a movement... Now let’s show the media how strong that movement is. Thank you, Donald J. Trump President of the United States
CONTRIBUTE $100 NOW >>>

name: Team Maggie
subject: FWD: Stop Steve Bannon & Donald Trump (It's go time) >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd69/2a380d31/15bf3a85/fee310/1654132353/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd69/2a380d31/15bf3a85/fee310/1654132353/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
We just pulled the numbers, team, and we need your help.
The first FEC deadline of the Trump era is TONIGHT. With Republicans bragging about how they're outraising Democrats by unprecedented margins, we HAVE to hit our $10,000 fundraising goal for Tammy Baldwin and Sherrod Brown.
Right now, we're coming up short.

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I need you to show it:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
The big fights will keep coming. We know that. So we need to keep working together, fighting together, and making one another stronger.
---
[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170331-Warren-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
Friend --

When we get out there and fight, we can make a real difference.

For seven years, the GOP talked a big game about repealing the Affordable Care Act. They said that ripping health care away from millions of people was their top priority in this new Congress. But we made calls, wrote letters, stood up in town halls and in the Capitol -- and they couldn't get it done.

Twice now, Trump has issued his unconstitutional, immoral Muslim ban. But we rallied in airports while the courts blocked him each time.

Look: the Republicans control the presidency, the House of Representatives, and the Senate. We can't win every fight. But despite that fact, we've shown that when we fight, we can win.

Senate Democrats are on the front lines as millions of people all across this country begin to stand up to Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell and the Republicans' dangerous agenda. So we need to keep working together, fighting together, and making one another stronger.

[**And the fundraising deadline we face tonight is a chance for us to show just how strong we can be. Will you chip in $1 and be part of it?**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170331-Warren-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

The big fights will keep coming. We know that.

Donald Trump and Senate Republicans will keep trying to defund Planned Parenthood. They'll keep trying to roll back protections for the environment, for workers, and for consumers. They'll keep trying to hand over the keys to our economy to the billionaires and giant corporations.

No matter how good it feels to stop one of Trump's terrible policies, we have to look ahead and be ready for what comes next.

I'm ready for each and every one of those fights -- and so are Senate Democrats all across this country. But we need you to stand with us.

**Chip in $1:**

[**http://dscc.org/democrats-fight-back**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170331-Warren-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Thank you for being part of this,

Elizabeth
**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e293/2b07114c/59bec6c5/736cb97c/259901959/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e293/2b07114c/59bec6c5/736cb97f/259901959/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e293/2b07114c/59bec6c5/736cb97e/259901959/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Judge Gorsuch
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

I have spent a lot of time over the last 6 months weighing the Supreme Court nomination of Judge Gorsuch. I met with him, read dozens of his opinions, and spoke to legal experts and lawyers who have appeared before his court. And I have heard a lot from my constituents. Based on this homework, I have decided to vote no on the nomination.

What stands out about Judge Gorsuch's history of rulings is his repeatedly activist approach to cases involving a woman's right to make her own decisions about her health.

Judge Gorsuch has taken extraordinary steps to force reconsideration of rulings that safeguard access to reproductive health care. He has argued that employers have a right to prevent their company's female employees from using _their own_ health insurance to buy contraception. And he has even referred to women making their own choices about contraception as "the wrongdoing of others."

**Derek, these rulings and statements do not demonstrate the type of judicial philosophy we need on the nation 's highest court. [Will you stand with me and call on the Senate to vote no on Judge Gorsuch?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=x1Z4xPk2dLURr4mpWoB4Wj5ePC8B1qHz)**

Many of Judge Gorsuch's other decisions have been equally troubling. He has ruled against workers' rights, disabled students, and so many other Americans who were only seeking fair treatment under the law.

**My first consideration is how Judge Gorsuch 's rulings would affect Virginians, and I am troubled by what I have seen in his record.** I am opposing Judge Gorsuch for the Supreme Court, and I'd like to know you have my back, Derek. **[Will you add your name to call on the Senate to vote no on Judge Gorsuch?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Sk176vkgaQllLWSVcUJEScB8jH8DMOYD)**

Thanks,
Tim
| [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Y53lW6pFJZZFO0Dd0JyiBj5ePC8B1qHz)
---
| Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=vwBOUkUDC%2FXJ3Z4t0vLAKz5ePC8B1qHz).
---

name: Joe Biden
subject: Hey - it's Joe
body:
Let's close March strong. I've been a public servant a long time. And even though my and Barack's time in the White House has come to an end, I still wake up every morning with a single thought: what can I do today to help make a difference?

That's been my guiding principle and the principle of Democrats like you all across the country. We lost big in November -- there's no pretending otherwise. But life isn't about how many times you get knocked down. It's about how many times you get back up.

So that one thing you can do today to help make a difference? Pitch in $3 or whatever you can right now before Democrats' first major quarterly fundraising deadline of the year to help keep this momentum going, because we've got a long road ahead.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaf/4067b0a/2e12d24ad/7e734a74/1865577989/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb_nd2&source=em_20170331_jb_nd2&refcode=em_20170331_jb_nd2")

[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaf/4067b0a/2e12d24ad/7e734a71/1865577989/VEsA/ "https://my.democrats.org/March-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb_nd2&source=em_20170331_jb_nd2&refcode=em_20170331_jb_nd2")
** Our resilience will carry us through to some major victories for every American as long as we just keep fighting. I know we can make this happen, together.

Thanks.

Joe

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaf/4067b0a/2e12d24ad/7e734a7e/1865577989/VEsB/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb_nd2&source=em_20170331_jb_nd2&refcode=em_20170331_jb_nd2"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaf/4067b0a/2e12d24ad/7e734a7f/1865577989/VEsO/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb_nd2&source=em_20170331_jb_nd2&refcode=em_20170331_jb_nd2"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaf/4067b0a/2e12d24ad/7e734a7c/1865577989/VEsP/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb_nd2&source=em_20170331_jb_nd2&refcode=em_20170331_jb_nd2") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaf/4067b0a/2e12d24ad/7e734a7d/1865577989/VEsHBQ/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb_nd2&source=em_20170331_jb_nd2&refcode=em_20170331_jb_nd2"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

|
clean_body: Let's close March strong. I've been a public servant a long time. And even though my and Barack's time in the White House has come to an end, I still wake up every morning with a single thought: what can I do today to help make a difference? That's been my guiding principle and the principle of Democrats like you all across the country. We lost big in November -- there's no pretending otherwise. But life isn't about how many times you get knocked down. It's about how many times you get back up. So that one thing you can do today to help make a difference? Pitch in $3 or whatever you can right now before Democrats' first major quarterly fundraising deadline of the year to help keep this momentum going, because we've got a long road ahead. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. "" "" "" "" "" "" Our resilience will carry us through to some major victories for every American as long as we just keep fighting. I know we can make this happen, together. Thanks. Joe | ---|--- Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

name: Caitlin Mitchell, EMILY's List
subject: Fwd: Why these ⑨ Senate women need us >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d8a/24c69fd9/f10972d5/18e3802a/2971775278/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE335N_digital_email_20170331_Kicker9Senate_inact&source=WQEFE335N_digital_email_20170331_Kicker9Senate_inact")
---

Peter,

OVER half of our EMILY's List women in the Senate are up for re-election next year.

And we're going to have to give it all we've got to make sure they stay there.

This community's already stepping up -- last night we hit our $180,000 goal for End-of-Quarter. We want to see if we can get to $220,000 before March is over.

**Donate $3 before the midnight deadline to keep these pro-choice Democratic women where they belong -- in the Senate, fighting President Trump and the GOP:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Before-March-Is-Over](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d8a/24c69fd9/f10972d5/18e3802b/2971775278/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170328_email_Over_Half_Our_Senate_Women?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgBeforeMarchIsOve&utm_campaign=WQEFE335N_digital_email_20170331_Kicker9Senate_inact&source=WQEFE335N_digital_email_20170331_Kicker9Senate_inact")**

Thanks,

Caitlin Mitchell
Vice President of Digital, EMILY's List

_\----------- Original Message -----------
From: EMILY's List
Sent: Tues, Mar 28, 2017 at 6:25 p.m._
[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7d8a/24c69fd9/f10972d5/18e38024/2971775278/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170328_email_Over_Half_Our_Senate_Women?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=WQEFE335N_digital_email_20170331_Kicker9Senate_inact&source=WQEFE335N_digital_email_20170331_Kicker9Senate_inact")

name: Pat Trueman
subject: We're on a deadline
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Virginia45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
---
| | | | | | [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014LFjpAi1LDK8-RYWxR-Yl9nFvjH5x60gxh59ufvVnWlAWT1Osec1wtQ6mzhzNQabMt_6Gkb92LdCc73QJTFMCeDd3h28OmJE-ocvs3lXa5rtHvNK-ZyILDAnLo4g7XLS1Rh9aks92U2mvJOR-iadYsEZ9r5lPinly-bEFznNYb2iwNAGexk_c7V5PpLs2wdzAzWUxD3De2G6ONRQtooQW6RNYbMruZpUiKr61gqCEh1xYoLzu1BxpQ==&c=_Bx02ZVshtfvG_s4A4hXapI8vXNoiSBzAgCl0Akx0tTUDYHVVHdSEw==&ch=ByoODrTs-W5snh1Ry9kBnRpd_jF4aH1vlUMKSvX-cIMquoBIk9_owQ==)
---
| | |
---
| Peter,

**We 're on a deadline, and I need your help.**
Midnight tonight is the close of Jill's 1st quarter, and my hope is to send a clear signal to the left: our campaign is equipped to win in November. But we can't do that without your help.
---

name: Tom Perez
subject: Our obligation to you
body:
Let's close March strong.
---
**Our first quarterly fundraising deadline of 2017 is tonight at midnight, and we need your help to make sure Democrats have a strong showing. Pitch in $10 or more right now.

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734ef1/2416835104/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/March-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem")**
When my friend Keith and I started out in our new roles, we knew that we had an obligation to make this party everything you think it can be.

Our party should reflect our values, our concerns, our accomplishments, and our strength. So when we hear about how a majority of Americans want the Affordable Care Act's reforms to stay in place, or demand action to stop gun violence, or support marriage equality -- we want the party that's fighting for those things to get stronger.

And that's exactly what we'll do. But here's the kicker:

As always, we face political fundraising and reporting deadlines. This first quarterly one of 2017 is tonight, and then we'll produce a public report showing what kind of shape our party is in. So, before the clock strikes midnight, will you pitch in $10 or whatever you can?

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $10](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734eff/2416835104/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+QUICK+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem")
[QUICK DONATE: $25](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734efc/2416835104/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+QUICK+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem")
[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734efd/2416835104/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734efa/2416835104/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem")
[QUICK DONATE: $200](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734efb/2416835104/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=200&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+QUICK+DONATE+200&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem")
[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734ef8/2416835104/VEsO/ "https://my.democrats.org/March-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem")
** Thanks. I'm truly grateful to be in this with you. It's our party -- we'll win elections if we work for it.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734e83/2416835104/VEsHAA/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=15+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734e80/2416835104/VEsHAw/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=16+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734e81/2416835104/VEsHAg/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=17+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caaa/4067b0a/2e0e2a0f3/7e734e8e/2416835104/VEsHDQ/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=18+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170331_tp2_rem&source=em_20170331_tp2_rem&refcode=em_20170331_tp2_rem"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: 31 days of people power
body:
This is how far we've come
#
Team -
Thirty-one days ago, I sent you an email asking for your support in our campaign to transform Florida into a state that works for all of us. There were a lot of folks who wrote us off immediately, and said that we wouldn't be able to raise the funds necessary to be competitive, that we wouldn't stand a chance against the entrenched powers that be. They underestimated us.
Over 3,000 of you stepped up and invested in our campaign online at an average contribution of just $50. 97% of you contributed less than $200. This is what a people-powered campaign looks like.
We've come this far in just 31 days -- and together, we're going to keep moving forward. Together, we're going to keep up this momentum until it brings us to victory, so we can bring it home for Florida.
If you haven't made a contribution to our insurgent campaign yet, this is your last chance to be listed on our very first campaign finance report, and be a part of this from the start. [**You can donate to our campaign right here, right now:**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445d/3904859233/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-march2017?refcode=em20170331-am")
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445d/3904859233/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-march2017?refcode=em20170331-am")**
Remember, this is just the beginning. Thank you for being a part of this -- I can't wait to see what we can accomplish together.
Bring it home,
Andrew

* * *
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Andrew Gillum
Date: Wed, Mar 1, 2017 at 8:00 AM @andrewgillum.com>

Dear Friend,
Growing up, my grandmother always told us to go to school, mind our teachers, get our lesson, and bring that education home - for our brothers, our baby sister, and the kids in our neighborhood.
She wanted us to know we were responsible for more than ourselves -- we are also responsible for our family and our neighborhood, and everyone around us.
My grandmother knew then what I know now -- that if we were going to get anywhere, we would get there together. And that's why I'm coming to you today, with a humble heart and a whole lot of hope, as we launch this campaign for Governor of Florida. Because I know that our only path to victory is one we're willing to travel together.
[**Add your name now to join our people powered campaign, and help us bring it home for Florida.**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445d/3904859233/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-march2017?refcode=em20170331-am")
Like so many of you, I am here today because of the very policies Republicans say are "destroying" our great state of Florida. I grew up on food stamps in Miami and Gainesville. My parents weren't college educated, but they both worked hard: my mother as a bus driver and my father as a construction worker. As Governor, I will work to create jobs that pay a living wage on every rung of the income ladder.
Our family struggled with being cash poor, but I still grew up rich in opportunity. Though the public schools I attended weren't always in the best part of town, they were always filled with the best teachers. I wouldn't be writing you today as a candidate for Governor of Florida had it not been for the encouragement and help I received from Ms. Alexandria at Westwood Middle School and Ms. Aubrey at Gainesville High School. They went above and beyond the requirements of their job, like so many teachers do, to ensure that I became the first person in my family to graduate college.
But I knew it wasn't enough for me to become the first in my family to go to college -- it was my duty to make sure I wasn't the last. The Democratic Party and progressive philosophy created a way up and a way out for even families like mine, and so when I began my career in public service at just 23 years old, I did so as a progressive Democrat. My experiences reflected those of average, everyday Floridians. And I knew those people deserved a champion.
For me, being a Democrat has always been about welcoming folks just as they are. This was the party that advocated for my parents growing up, that didn't demonize them or label them as lazy. Instead, they fought for progress for all of us. And that's what I've worked to do in my time on the Tallahassee City Council and as Mayor of Tallahassee.
When marriage equality was won at the Federal level and we had Florida county after county after county saying that they would no longer issue marriage licenses to anybody, just so they didn't have to do it for gay couples. So I welcomed couples to our great city of Tallahassee to celebrate their love and commitment to one another with marriage.
When President Trump's executive order targeted immigrants in our cities, I protested loudly and proudly, and fought to stop him from tearing these families apart. This concept of having to choose between security and compassion is absolutely false. We can have both, and we must fight for both.
In fact, I have worked tirelessly to make Tallahassee a safe haven for all -- and I have done so without resorting to irrational discrimination. When I refused to repeal ordinances that prevent shooting guns in a public park, the gun lobby sued me. They thought they could intimidate me with threats of personal fines, with two years of litigation, and with attacks from right-wing interest groups. They were wrong. Just last month, we won the battle to keep our parks safe.
My pastor talks about it in the terms of being the thermostat versus the thermometer. There are enough people who are prepared to take the temperature. There are not enough people who are willing to set it. I think I've demonstrated up to this point that I've never had to take the temperature in order to do the right thing.
I believe we are at a time in our state and our nation's history that requires not just people who quietly agree on these critical issues we are facing, but people who are going to be champions, who will get out and lead on them.
That's why I'm asking you to join together with us from the very beginning, to help us prove that we have that power, that hope, and that determination to rebuild this state into one that works for all of us, no matter what we look like, where we come from, what we believe, or whom we love.
[**Let us know if you 'll be joining together with us for this historic campaign by adding your name right here and right now.**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445d/3904859233/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-march2017?refcode=em20170331-am")
Thank you for being a part of this journey. I look forward to traveling the state and meeting with you and the rest of the team in the coming weeks.
Bring it home,
Mayor Andrew Gillum
Candidate for Florida Governor

**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445d/3904859233/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-march2017?refcode=em20170331-am")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445c/3904859233/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445f/3904859233/VEsB/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb33a6/9e00690/498555a8/2403445e/3904859233/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

There were a lot of folks who wrote us off immediately, and said that we wouldn't be able to raise the funds necessary to be competitive, that we wouldn't stand a chance against the entrenched powers that be. They underestimated us. Over 3,000 of you stepped up and invested in our campaign online at an average contribution of just $50. 97% of you contributed less than $200. subject: The Final Stretch
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| |
---
|
---
**_The Final Stretch_**
We're approaching the final three weeks of this year's General Assembly and from all indications Governor Eric Holcomb, Speaker Brian Bosma, Senator David Long and our Republican super-majorities in the House and Senate are working overtime to make good on a series of transformative promises that will accelerate our economic momentum, curb the drug epidemic and deliver a long-term plan to maintain and grow our state's infrastructure. Proven leadership brings proven results. Year after year after year.

\- Kyle
(Photo: Chairman Hupfer spent his Friday lunch hour at State Party HQ with a group of 30 Indiana Senate interns discussing the role of State Party and our priorities for the 2018 election cycle.)
|
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
|
---
---
| Governor Holcomb this week [spoke at the annual Leadership Prayer Breakfast](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0214fb192d&e=1acd9bb3d6) while Indiana's First Dog, Henry Holcomb, [officially received his Statehouse badge](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f2c66314c0&e=1acd9bb3d6), the governor's [dancing skills](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ede4f70edc&e=1acd9bb3d6) earned him an honorary membership in the Indiana Black Legislative Caucus and the governor [held a press conference](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=05dadccc44&e=1acd9bb3d6) to discuss the latest on his Next Level Legislative Agenda.
---
|
---
| **News You Can Use**
Governor Eric Holcomb Op-Ed: [Act Now to Make Indiana a Tech Capital](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5e35f16e0e&e=1acd9bb3d6)
Attorney General Curtis Hill [Walked a Neighborhood with Ten Point Coalition Members and IMPD](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3aaa00206c&e=1acd9bb3d6)
Congressman Todd Rokita's [Bill Signed Into Law by President Trump](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2169231f66&e=1acd9bb3d6)
Congresswoman Susan Brooks [on Indiana Leading the Way with Next Generation 911](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3ba56fd318&e=1acd9bb3d6)
Congressman Luke Messer [Calls for More Work on Obamacare Repeal](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=08b9b69e19&e=1acd9bb3d6)
Congressman Larry Bucshon [Met with Leaders from Ortho Indy & Pinnacle Hospital in D.C.](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ecb98bc450&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Events Calendar**

[Saturday, April 8](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=90e620c651&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- DeKalb County Lincoln Day with Gov. Eric Holcomb

[Thursday, April 20](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2eac8244a0&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- St. Joseph County Lincoln Day with Congressman Luke Messer
[Thursday, April 20](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=37d9f3be09&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- Adams County Lincoln Day with Secretary of State Connie Lawson & Treasurer Kelly Mitchell
[Saturday, April 22](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d0d57db06d&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- Starke County Lincoln Day
[Sunday, April 23](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6e6e54b0d4&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- Parke County Lincoln Day with Attorney General Curtis Hill
[Monday, April 24](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d986a7921b&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- Clay County Lincoln Day with Secretary of State Connie Lawson

name: Chuck Schumer
subject: re: tonight's deadline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
At midnight, we'll know whether or not this team did what it's going to take to hit our goals. So we're counting on our best supporters to step up.
---
[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170331-Schumer-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
Friend --

Tonight, we face a test.

In just a few hours, we'll hit our first big fundraising deadline of the year.

At midnight, we'll know whether or not this team did what it's going to take to hit our goals.

And in the morning, we'll be able to dive into the numbers and know how much we'll be able to do in the weeks ahead to hold Republicans accountable and defend the Democrats who are fighting for our values.

[**So before this deadline, I'm asking you to chip in $1.**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170331-Schumer-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

We're only $154,426 away from our goal.

That means, we only need about 5,800 more contributions to put us over the top.

So we're counting on our best supporters to step up.

And to make the decision easier for you, every contribution before midnight will be triple-matched.

Even $1 will make a big difference.

So make a donation before midnight:

[**http://dscc.org/democrats-fight-back**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dsccab4?refcode=DPE-20170331-Schumer-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Thank you,

Chuck
**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e2a2/2b07114c/59e7e0fd/736caa53/1787167499/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e2a2/2b07114c/59e7e0fd/736caa52/1787167499/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e2a2/2b07114c/59e7e0fd/736caa4d/1787167499/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). We greatly appreciate your continued support. Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

name: New Hampshire Republican Party
subject: Your Weekly NH GOP Newsletter
body:
| |
---
| 31
**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, MARCH 31st |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
This past week was busy and productive! At the State House, we took a great step forward in assuring that every vote counts with Senate Bill 3. Since 2012, more than 2,200 voter verification letters have been returned as undeliverable. In the last election alone, 458 verification letters were returned.
[**_SB3, Senator Regina Birdsell's bill_**](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbT0pW52ma3LTMrghVm-2FI6cWOrzttPYXIL8ZgL2Ta1Vr7lwn9kLeyG-2Bu4VusIGGPWcVWGwgt3fKjTxvb-2Fo1ij3yBArxasJn-2FidHvmeypZUmaRRkPJ7cAYXBnpeJa9Tcp89RC2g7B7-2Fuo2uKuNduR0l5ajXfq0g5Ky8m6gJIatVT-2FA_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLEVlDSJ1MefXX8r0gYUINJPStwOHt25T-2BxmCXqxaLcl-2FmQFYHNTV4OCMIEt6hzWV32gC5YRNyjDFlkKQySlxcJIUoooU64kKPMPJc9-2FOeC0KkqGAMV9qaNj0PoizL-2BVlqkx876DziTTk49V6xg9ERCwvdEI5WrY2ldGKdBf1ZU4eItgGdZcnT7jvu-2FIaskvIQe6Rsnc-2FoBFxXXVo2Z8o5fU5q4djzeGEuXnm6ml8ucrzBatzfJvvzUWHkY4hS9tdavtd3uIK7WeAP1pBNrzZxw-3D-3D), better defines domicile for voting purposes. This bill strengthens voter laws and protects the rights of NH voters. _**[Secretary of State Gardner supports this bill](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbY1qiyT3HuRDH6pVWPAnYR6FcHAlAfrDanC8C-2BXpcAouJAl0fcAGG5bhZiwZm6okwTo-2B3QKalUiyXe7RF8MPGNYb9EEuKF-2BRnc7wnzZWdv-2Bnt31TBQQrcH4Z0TzXn-2FI5Vg-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLEVlDSJ1MefXX8r0gYUINJPStwOHt25T-2BxmCXqxaLcl-2FmQFYHNTV4OCMIEt6hzWV32gC5YRNyjDFlkKQySlxcGDLVsi9Loebz9oTAeeCRdtKOjh6rRNaszjM94ohGDnA9zZTVlWcNUCtgu8rpCJgb6Aj79dQKb7YvfYHmcqmw-2Fvrx9cHOKOIb5DFlW81x-2BWtxnsuX5UhS-2BNZTw2af07UyUCHdPZLREDxY1Ju8b1JKxsLiXa0gf4ToawUtBim9w56VWTx-2BL7XpjhokA6pE-2B7-2Bpw-3D-3D)**_ because it takes the position of trust but verify. Unfortunately, but not surprising, the Democrats voted against safeguarding this most precious right. We expect this timely piece of legislation will quickly pass the House and be signed into law by Governor Sununu.
Whether approving a bill to fund the _**[crossborder drug interdiction program](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2Bbcnlc7KWqHwzevwvBy6AF9UnVrHBAhXMvF1NK8hpQU72iHMm5bFVjkioeI0vWAo94qLNBmStxpD3wyC4UxGy3NOwBBblSghmSvbePDGxk1wKjMRbQb1YSBlk2RC4sjOA6K8078zy2XrQ334i2I2LjDA-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLEVlDSJ1MefXX8r0gYUINJPStwOHt25T-2BxmCXqxaLcl-2FmQFYHNTV4OCMIEt6hzWV32gC5YRNyjDFlkKQySlxcJUikfOaqRh-2BRbOVskeuMRLfaiwrtTzD-2Ba7MpF0LXLysNqA5Il1Ciwz04dvGg-2FKaaWbjcerhDfxm4QXDTj1K8nhh0NtL3q4UGrQZjdkMzpxyNfBkc5Cwzv-2FaESy73LmfHTWRGPG1GvWMygTtk-2B5BRl2CHHN-2B8U9H6aI-2BZ36O4gmuFS4TCcTSrf0lyr8nLFGLCA-3D-3D)**_ , or providing _**[disaster relief caused by the 2016 drought](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbR-2BncdYrjYGa-2BcdGvpvk-2BU2Bg9PVAcpW8Lj5lVCmMxnrSd3Z8FLjt9ciKKMNPtVIIUG4U5pliaFnyWkppMfnM0VdInAxaV30euIFnBq037FLUNLteTlBy124UetWEi-2Fl4IYC5hf1x28pxFQpORlLAco-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLEVlDSJ1MefXX8r0gYUINJPStwOHt25T-2BxmCXqxaLcl-2FmQFYHNTV4OCMIEt6hzWV32gC5YRNyjDFlkKQySlxcCslCDX05xAcHlaSI9qHNuKj373cx00t-2FhEa7yk6G6DfvU6n56RHwKSciwPIcqxEqM9SnI2wN-2BRs8pAgOEygfXZl-2BfCljLpkPl52pyDL6fJrwf-2BEgzXt8xfcjsKy9P9J6BrbFVTG2wW4qHO0c31o4sMJqW6-2BOUXFkv6A-2FiTuTeUsDq-2BRvF-2FD8LAFaWN3hcpj-2FQ-3D-3D)**_ , the House and Senate are working hand-in-hand with the Governor. This is the kind of successful collaboration you can expect with our Republican majority – working together to advance an agenda that supports our hard-working families, communities, and businesses.
The Republican Party is a party of openness and transparency, and you won’t see us passing legislation in the dark of night. You will see us debating the issues, because we are open and transparent. But you will also see us, at the end of the day, coming together and moving forward, always putting New Hampshire citizens first.
Also this week, President and Mrs. Trump hosted a _**[Women's Empowerment Panel](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyOjIesYJFWT3v-2BLoSZZM67BPFyE47QaJ8f3cFL8aku8F2gQ501Gg0Djp9-2BVMGNJF7cj4QiwDPFsPUOn9YHu-2BhuKlVrVK8DKun93r4UZdOb5iqgoXKuPt2h2mNStciJjPGg-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLEVlDSJ1MefXX8r0gYUINJPStwOHt25T-2BxmCXqxaLcl-2FmQFYHNTV4OCMIEt6hzWV32gC5YRNyjDFlkKQySlxcN8dStrSX5POj0bePSIMlA-2BC-2B22XcLYdVLP-2FmrN5MShnVa1LYPZZCxY6PY-2BbFx-2BEGEX78mEDEbgQid5gnp28d8c73KezbOGtaCjyn1mQTP0VotevwgXeptLKdfhti-2BdzsdSTyAPXSiLxaSbwnqhHCxNzlfnVDnLoVLj-2F2-2FmWBlXWsSZnfCGqZvxP2oFiNDbNnw-3D-3D)**_ at the White House, in recognition of Women's History Month. We got to hear first hand from leaders like Betsy DeVos (Secretary of Education), Linda McMahon (Administrator of the SBA), Nikki Haley (US Ambassador to the UN) and Seema Verma (Administrator of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services).
Finally, this week we've confirmed that a very special high-profile guest from Washington will join us on May 18th at our first fundraiser of the year. Stay tuned for more details!

Sincerely,
_Jeanie_

|
---
---
---
---

| Matthew Boyle, 29, has been the Washington Political Editor for Breitbart News since October 2015, overseeing all U.S. political coverage for the news service. He joined Breitbart News as a political reporter in December 2012, after spending two and a half years as an investigative reporter for The Daily Caller. A graduate of Flagler College in St. Augustine, Florida, with a bachelor's degree in communications with an emphasis in journalism, Boyle grew up in Canton, Massachusetts. He lived in Idaho and Minnesota during his college years before finishing his degree in Florida, and moving to Washington, D.C. in mid-2010. As a national political journalist, he has been ahead of the curve on many major stories including the election of Donald Trump to the presidency in 2016, the defeat of Eric Cantor in 2014, the resignation of former Speaker John Boehner in 2015, the failure of the 2013 U.S. Senate "Gang of Eight" amnesty bill, and many more national issues. His reporting has led to the indictment of U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez, the conviction of State Department contractors involved in illegally steering millions of dollars in government contracts, and the resignations of many government, campaign and other officials for corruption. He has interviewed President Trump in the Oval Office, and conducted many more high profile interviews including with then-candidate Trump, then Vice Presidential candidate Mike Pence, many other presidential candidates, many U.S. Senators, governors and congressmen, as well as key movement and cultural leaders. His narrative driving reporting on major political issues of the day is regularly featured on many major national news programs and outlets, including the Drudge Report, Rush Limbaugh Show, Hannity, Mark Levin Show, and more. President Trump has called him a "great reporter." |

| _**House Republicans**_
**House Majority Leader’s Statement on the Passage SB3**
CONCORD- House Majority Leader Richard “Dick” Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement regarding the Senate's passage of SB3 relative to domicile for voting purposes.
“I applaud our Republican colleagues in the Senate today on the passage of Senate Bill 3. SB3 is an important piece of legislation that will continue to preserve New Hampshire’s electoral integrity by making sure that the right to vote is protected for all of the eligible voters of our state while cracking down on those that look to take advantage of the voting process.
In a state where numerous elections have been decided by just a handful of votes, it is important to make sure that every ballot cast by an eligible voter is counted. I look forward to SB3’s introduction to the House and working to get it passed so we can get this important piece of legislation to the Governor's desk.”
|

| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Senate backs food stamp bill, adds workforce development program**
Concord, NH - Today, the Senate passed a bill, SB 7, which would strengthen New Hampshire’s food stamp program and establish the Granite Workforce pilot program.
The Granite Workforce program would use allowable excess reserve funds from the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) fund to be used for workforce development and helping citizens overcome barriers to work like transportation, childcare and education or skills training. After removing barriers to work, participants will be placed in unfilled jobs, providing our services to New Hampshire employers who are struggling to fill these positions.
Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua), the bill’s prime sponsor, issued the following statement: “The goal of this legislation is to restore the Federal eligibility standard for food stamps in New Hampshire, while creating a stronger program that helps those truly in need and does not negatively impact our most vulnerable children and families.
“This bill aims to help people return to the workforce through work requirements as well as a basic asset test in order to receive food stamps. Establishing a work requirement will enable able bodied, childless adults who are currently on food stamps to become increasingly self-reliant rather than being dependent on the government while helping to fill thousands of open jobs in New Hampshire.”
“These measures are designed to help low-income participants break the cycle of poverty and move them from living on the margin to the middle class and beyond.”
**Background:** Granite Workforce program outlined in this bill also creates partnerships to address specific barriers including substance abuse, a lack of consistent childcare, in addition to skills training and education among others. In addition, this measure creates an employer reimbursement benefit which will encourage employers to hire and retain individuals within the Granite Workforce program specifically. |

| **WOMEN’S POLITICAL LEADERSHIP INSTITUTE ANNOUNCES** **2017-2018 VESTA ROY SERIES** The Women’s Political Leadership Institute is pleased to announce the 2017-2018 Vesta Roy Series. The Series has been completely revised to address the skills necessary to be successful politically. Classes will be held on Saturdays in order to accommodate the needs of women who work outside of the home or are busy raising families at home during the week. Session topics will include: Team Building, including self-presentation in the media; New Hampshire politics; Media and Communication; and Leadership Toolbox, including tools necessary to run a successful campaign. The Saturday sessions will be held on October 14 and November 4, 2017, and on March 3 and April 7, 2018, at 2 Pillsbury St. in Concord from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. at a location to be announced. A light lunch and beverages will be provided. In the event of snow, the class will be moved to the following Saturday. Fellows are expected to attend all four Saturday sessions unless there are extenuating circumstances. In addition, there will be an optional day at the State House on a Thursday in February, 2018, that all Fellows are encouraged to attend unless they have a job conflict.
There will be an optional trip to Washington, DC in May, 2018, for the annual Excellence in Public Service Series national meeting as well as meetings with elected and other officials in Washington. Fellows will pay their own airfare and incidentals for the DC trip.
The fee for the Series is $200. Fellows are expected to work as a team to plan and execute a major fundraiser in the year following their graduation. They are also expected to act as an ambassador for the program and to assist with recruiting at least one new applicant the year following their graduation. All active registered Republican women who would like to run for office, apply for political appointments, manage campaigns, or engage more actively in other political activities in New Hampshire are strongly encouraged to apply. Applications are available on the website (**_[wpli.org](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbQtMd1UqLImw01UJIefNxgg-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MLEVlDSJ1MefXX8r0gYUINJPStwOHt25T-2BxmCXqxaLcl-2FmQFYHNTV4OCMIEt6hzWV32gC5YRNyjDFlkKQySlxcFZyuh5sffRveQWpAh2QiOhBcquAZY5EMyS3ady-2BqBMNK4lFg27uCe9iGcdTV3TqoL0FTyG6630LDuL-2FKR0l-2Fqi4VNwnFxxCvKJoHIuj2tSbqBxd5ikHTAYrYkTFrgqoIMxKqp0gmS8GsGiF5J40pzp0UjfbhtQe-2Ftv-2Ft1czUMGogiS8FwICqdfmcgaWsEiICg-3D-3D)_**) and are due by May 15. Interviews will be conducted during the first week in June and decisions mailed on June 15.
WPLI Board members include: Robin Anderson, Margaret Drye, Jan Face Glassman, Suzanne Lehman, Leann Moccia, Beth Perlo, Karen Provencher, Donna Sytek, Katy Sullivan, and Joanne Ward.
