clean_body: | | | 31 NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, MARCH 31st | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, This past week was busy and productive! At the State House, we took a great step forward in assuring that every vote counts with Senate Bill 3. Since 2012, more than 2,200 voter verification letters have been returned as undeliverable. In the last election alone, 458 verification letters were returned. , better defines domicile for voting purposes. This bill strengthens voter laws and protects the rights of NH voters. because it takes the position of trust but verify. Unfortunately, but not surprising, the Democrats voted against safeguarding this most precious right. We expect this timely piece of legislation will quickly pass the House and be signed into law by Governor Sununu. Whether approving a bill to fund the , or providing , the House and Senate are working hand-in-hand with the Governor. This is the kind of successful collaboration you can expect with our Republican majority – working together to advance an agenda that supports our hard-working families, communities, and businesses. The Republican Party is a party of openness and transparency, and you won’t see us passing legislation in the dark of night. You will see us debating the issues, because we are open and transparent. But you will also see us, at the end of the day, coming together and moving forward, always putting New Hampshire citizens first. Also this week, President and Mrs. Trump hosted a at the White House, in recognition of Women's History Month. We got to hear first hand from leaders like Betsy DeVos (Secretary of Education), Linda McMahon (Administrator of the SBA), Nikki Haley (US Ambassador to the UN) and Seema Verma (Administrator of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services). Finally, this week we've confirmed that a very special high-profile guest from Washington will join us on May 18th at our first fundraiser of the year. Stay tuned for more details! Sincerely, Jeanie | | | Join the Grassroots Circle! | | The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. | | | | Intern with the NH GOP! | | Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. | | | Upcoming Events | ---|---|--- | Amherst GOP Monthly Meeting April 1 - 8:00 AM Joey's Diner, 1 Craftsman Lane, Amherst, NH NH Young Republican Annual Meeting April 1 - 1:00 PM Shaskeen Pub, 909 Elm St, Manchester, NH For more information Manchester GOP Monthly Meeting April 3 - 6:00 PM Murphy's Taproom, 494 Elm Street, Manchester, NH Pelham GOP Monthly Meeting April 3 - 7:00 PM Pelham VFW, 6 Main Street, Pelham, NH Rockingham County GOP Exec. Board Meeting April 5 - 8:00 AM Hilton-Brentwood, 117 North Road, Brentwood, NH Buffet and Presentation with Senator Bob Giuda April 5 - 6:00 pM Top of the Town Restaurant, 88 Ladd Hill Road, Belmont, NH For more information **NHGOP Social Media & Messaging Training April 29th - St. Anselms College - Institute of Politics, 100 St. Anselm Drive, Manchester, NH For more information, see below or Save the Date! - NHGOP "Spring to Victory" Dinner w/Special Guest TBA May 18 - 5:30 PM Nashua, NH | | | | | | We are excited to have Matthew Boyle from Breitbart News join us on April 29th for our grassroots training. | ---|---|--- | Matthew Boyle, 29, has been the Washington Political Editor for Breitbart News since October 2015, overseeing all U.S. political coverage for the news service. He joined Breitbart News as a political reporter in December 2012, after spending two and a half years as an investigative reporter for The Daily Caller. A graduate of Flagler College in St. Augustine, Florida, with a bachelor's degree in communications with an emphasis in journalism, Boyle grew up in Canton, Massachusetts. He lived in Idaho and Minnesota during his college years before finishing his degree in Florida, and moving to Washington, D.C. in mid-2010. As a national political journalist, he has been ahead of the curve on many major stories including the election of Donald Trump to the presidency in 2016, the defeat of Eric Cantor in 2014, the resignation of former Speaker John Boehner in 2015, the failure of the 2013 U.S. Senate "Gang of Eight" amnesty bill, and many more national issues. His reporting has led to the indictment of U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez, the conviction of State Department contractors involved in illegally steering millions of dollars in government contracts, and the resignations of many government, campaign and other officials for corruption. He has interviewed President Trump in the Oval Office, and conducted many more high profile interviews including with then-candidate Trump, then Vice Presidential candidate Mike Pence, many other presidential candidates, many U.S. Senators, governors and congressmen, as well as key movement and cultural leaders. His narrative driving reporting on major political issues of the day is regularly featured on many major national news programs and outlets, including the Drudge Report, Rush Limbaugh Show, Hannity, Mark Levin Show, and more. President Trump has called him a "great reporter." | ---|---|--- | | Photo of the Week | ---|---|--- Senator Sharon Carson & Chairman Jeanie Forrester in DC this Wednesday for the "Women's Empowerment Panel" at the White House | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight shines on the . We applaud Senator Birdsell for her outstanding work on SB3, which helps strengthen our voting laws here in New Hampshire. Her bill passed in the Senate and is heading to the House where it is expected to pass and then quickly signed into law by the Governor. Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans House Majority Leader’s Statement on the Passage SB3 CONCORD- House Majority Leader Richard “Dick” Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement regarding the Senate's passage of SB3 relative to domicile for voting purposes. “I applaud our Republican colleagues in the Senate today on the passage of Senate Bill 3. SB3 is an important piece of legislation that will continue to preserve New Hampshire’s electoral integrity by making sure that the right to vote is protected for all of the eligible voters of our state while cracking down on those that look to take advantage of the voting process. In a state where numerous elections have been decided by just a handful of votes, it is important to make sure that every ballot cast by an eligible voter is counted. I look forward to SB3’s introduction to the House and working to get it passed so we can get this important piece of legislation to the Governor's desk.” | | In memory of 10-term State Representative Steve Vaillancourt, members of the House placed a Plymouth State University hat at his seat on the Finance Committee. | | Senate Republicans Senate backs food stamp bill, adds workforce development program Concord, NH - Today, the Senate passed a bill, SB 7, which would strengthen New Hampshire’s food stamp program and establish the Granite Workforce pilot program. The Granite Workforce program would use allowable excess reserve funds from the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) fund to be used for workforce development and helping citizens overcome barriers to work like transportation, childcare and education or skills training. After removing barriers to work, participants will be placed in unfilled jobs, providing our services to New Hampshire employers who are struggling to fill these positions. Senator Kevin Avard (R-Nashua), the bill’s prime sponsor, issued the following statement: “The goal of this legislation is to restore the Federal eligibility standard for food stamps in New Hampshire, while creating a stronger program that helps those truly in need and does not negatively impact our most vulnerable children and families. “This bill aims to help people return to the workforce through work requirements as well as a basic asset test in order to receive food stamps. Establishing a work requirement will enable able bodied, childless adults who are currently on food stamps to become increasingly self-reliant rather than being dependent on the government while helping to fill thousands of open jobs in New Hampshire.” “These measures are designed to help low-income participants break the cycle of poverty and move them from living on the margin to the middle class and beyond.” Background: Granite Workforce program outlined in this bill also creates partnerships to address specific barriers including substance abuse, a lack of consistent childcare, in addition to skills training and education among others. In addition, this measure creates an employer reimbursement benefit which will encourage employers to hire and retain individuals within the Granite Workforce program specifically. | | NHGOP Press**Releases | ---|---|--- | March 28 March 30 | | Vesta Roy Series 2017-2018 | ---|---|--- | WOMEN’S POLITICAL LEADERSHIP INSTITUTE ANNOUNCES 2017-2018 VESTA ROY SERIES The Women’s Political Leadership Institute is pleased to announce the 2017-2018 Vesta Roy Series. The Series has been completely revised to address the skills necessary to be successful politically. Classes will be held on Saturdays in order to accommodate the needs of women who work outside of the home or are busy raising families at home during the week. Session topics will include: Team Building, including self-presentation in the media; New Hampshire politics; Media and Communication; and Leadership Toolbox, including tools necessary to run a successful campaign. The Saturday sessions will be held on October 14 and November 4, 2017, and on March 3 and April 7, 2018, at 2 Pillsbury St. in Concord from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. at a location to be announced. A light lunch and beverages will be provided. In the event of snow, the class will be moved to the following Saturday. Fellows are expected to attend all four Saturday sessions unless there are extenuating circumstances. In addition, there will be an optional day at the State House on a Thursday in February, 2018, that all Fellows are encouraged to attend unless they have a job conflict. There will be an optional trip to Washington, DC in May, 2018, for the annual Excellence in Public Service Series national meeting as well as meetings with elected and other officials in Washington. Fellows will pay their own airfare and incidentals for the DC trip. The fee for the Series is $200. Fellows are expected to work as a team to plan and execute a major fundraiser in the year following their graduation. They are also expected to act as an ambassador for the program and to assist with recruiting at least one new applicant the year following their graduation. All active registered Republican women who would like to run for office, apply for political appointments, manage campaigns, or engage more actively in other political activities in New Hampshire are strongly encouraged to apply. Applications are available on the website () and are due by May 15. Interviews will be conducted during the first week in June and decisions mailed on June 15. WPLI Board members include: Robin Anderson, Margaret Drye, Jan Face Glassman, Suzanne Lehman, Leann Moccia, Beth Perlo, Karen Provencher, Donna Sytek, Katy Sullivan, and Joanne Ward. Further information is available on the website at . | | Empowering Republican Women! | ---|---|--- A message from RNC Chairwoman Ronna Romney McDaniel on Women's Empowerment | This Week's Best Tweets! | ---|---|--- | | | Tweet With Us! | | ---|---|--- | Connect with your Republican Leaders! | ---|---|--- | | | | | Speaker of the House Shawn Jasper | | | Senate President Chuck Morse | | | Executive Council | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | | | Governor Chris Sununu | | | Chairman Forrester & The NH GOP | ---|---|--- | | Web - "LIKE" on Facebook \- "FOLLOW" on Twitter - | | | Web - Facebook \- Twitter - Chairman Jeanie Forrester "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - "FOLLOW" The Staff on Twitter Finance Director - Political Director - | | 10 Water Street, Concord, NH 03301 Phone: (603) 225-9341 | Email: info@nhgop.org | | | ---|---|--- | | mailto:? | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire · 10 Water Street · Concord · New Hampshire · 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBE_ALT--

This isn't a legal requirement, but it's one of our core values: that dark money doesn't belong in politics. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf46d3/1c469400/2d5c617/3548bcd/512785125/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/legitaction_donate?refcode=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3&source=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3") Friend, I want to tell you something very important about LegitAction. We're planning to voluntarily disclose every donation we receive. This isn't a legal requirement, but it's one of our core values: that dark money doesn't belong in politics. Only by being transparent can our politics become legitimate again. As someone who's already stepped up with a donation to LegitAction, I am sincerely grateful for your support. [_**Make a transparent contribution of $3 to LegitAction before the clock strikes midnight to help us reach LegitAction 's goal and begin restoring legitimacy to our politics.**_](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf46d3/1c469400/2d5c617/3548bca/512785125/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/legitaction_donate?utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=2+-+Make+a+transparent+contribution+of+3+to+&utm_campaign=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3&source=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3") [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf46d3/1c469400/2d5c617/3548bcb/512785125/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/legitaction_donate?refcode=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3&source=20170331_r4w_EOQ1_msg3") Right now, we're $70,960 of the way to our $75,000 goal. name: Maggie Hassan
subject: 1,017 votes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello --
I'm proud to write you as the U.S. Senator from New Hampshire after our hard-fought victory in 2016.
You and the DSCC helped make it possible. As dark money groups overwhelmingly targeted our campaign -- making the race to unseat Kelly Ayotte one of the most expensive ever -- you helped grassroots Democrats prevail.
And because you stood with me, we won by 1,017 votes. That narrow margin is a reminder that we can never get complacent when the stakes are so high.
So today, I'm asking you step up like you did for me to make sure that 25 members of the Democratic caucus defend their seats in 2018 (including 10 in states that Donald Trump won).
**Will you join me in supporting the DSCC's work to keep Democrats fighting in the Senate, please?[Please, pitch in whatever you can before tonight's FEC end-of-quarter deadline.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd66/2a380d31/15bcdd53/fee3a2/202130295/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoibWFyY2gyMDE3LWRzY2MiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDMzMV9EU0NDYnVuZGxlX2FsbCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/march2017-dscc?refcode=e20170331_DSCCbundle_all&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")**
You and I both know that we can't afford to lose any of our progressive champions. Senate Democrats are holding the last, best line of defense against Trump's and Mitch McConnell's extreme policies.
That's exactly why right-wing groups are deploying upwards of $10 million on the Supreme Court fight, and it's why Trump is on the campaign trail lobbying mega-donors for a filibuster-proof majority.
It doesn't surprise me -- I saw firsthand the staggering amounts that GOP billionaires will spend to drown out anyone who stands in the way of their agenda.
The only way we keep our Senate Democrats fighting for hard-working families is to be stronger -- and to make sure that we band together to move razor-thin races toward Democratic victory in 2018.
[**I'm not taking anything for granted, everyone, so I'm standing with the DSCC to protect my fellow Democratic Senators in the fights ahead. Can I count on you to join me before tonight's FEC deadline?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd66/2a380d31/15bcdd53/fee3a2/202130295/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoibWFyY2gyMDE3LWRzY2MiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDMzMV9EU0NDYnVuZGxlX2FsbCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/march2017-dscc?refcode=e20170331_DSCCbundle_all&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
Thanks for everything you do.
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan
[Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd66/2a380d31/15bcdd53/fee3a2/202130295/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoibWFyY2gyMDE3LWRzY2MiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE3MDMzMV9EU0NDYnVuZGxlX2FsbCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/march2017-dscc?refcode=e20170331_DSCCbundle_all&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
To unsubscribe from the Maggie for NH email list, please click here: [Unsubscribe](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd66/2a380d31/15bcdd53/fee3a3/202130295/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

Paid for by Maggie for NH

Contributions or gifts to Maggie for NH are not tax deductible.

Maggie for NH
PO Box 298
Concord, NH 03302 United States

name: Joe Biden
subject: Hey - it's Joe
body:
Let's close March strong. I've been a public servant a long time. And even though my and Barack's time in the White House has come to an end, I still wake up every morning with a single thought: what can I do today to help make a difference?

That's been my guiding principle and the principle of Democrats like you all across the country. We lost big in November -- there's no pretending otherwise. But life isn't about how many times you get knocked down. It's about how many times you get back up.

So that one thing you can do today to help make a difference? Pitch in $3 or whatever you can right now before Democrats' first major quarterly fundraising deadline of the year to help keep this momentum going, because we've got a long road ahead.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caa1/4067b0a/2e01d1959/7e7334e8/2704710249/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&source=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&refcode=em_20170331_jb2_ndno")
[QUICK DONATE: $10](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caa1/4067b0a/2e01d1959/7e7334e9/2704710249/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+QUICK+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&source=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&refcode=em_20170331_jb2_ndno")
[QUICK DONATE: $25](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caa1/4067b0a/2e01d1959/7e7334f6/2704710249/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+QUICK+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&source=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&refcode=em_20170331_jb2_ndno")
[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caa1/4067b0a/2e01d1959/7e7334f7/2704710249/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&source=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&refcode=em_20170331_jb2_ndno")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caa1/4067b0a/2e01d1959/7e7334f4/2704710249/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dnc-march-deadline?express_lane=true&amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&source=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&refcode=em_20170331_jb2_ndno")
[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caa1/4067b0a/2e01d1959/7e7334f5/2704710249/VEsA/ "https://my.democrats.org/March-Deadline-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&source=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&refcode=em_20170331_jb2_ndno")
** Our resilience will carry us through to some major victories for every American as long as we just keep fighting. I know we can make this happen, together.

Thanks.

Joe

This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052caa1/4067b0a/2e01d1959/7e7334f1/2704710249/VEsHBQ/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&source=em_20170331_jb2_ndno&refcode=em_20170331_jb2_ndno"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.
